quote_of_day.text:
>>8125
>living in some rural village deep hidden in the hills would be better than this
it kinda is until you realize there's absolutely jack shit to do out in the boonies and you'll probably die out of boredom
still much better than soulless concrete jungles somehow
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of infatuation settled over the board, as Anon became enamored with a customer care voice, sparking a lengthy and fawning discussion. This warm front brought a mix of Hindi and English, with Anon smitten by the gentle tone.

A speculative fog rolled in, as Anon pondered the physical appearance of IAC, trading humorous barbs and lighthearted jabs. Meanwhile, a cold snap of contrition hit, when Anon apologized for anti-Dalit posts, only to be met with a backlash of criticism.

Scattered showers of sexism and anti-Semitism also swept across the board. Anon sparked debate with inflammatory comments about 'pajeeta' women, likening them unfavorably to Jews.

Elsewhere, a lone voice proposed new features for the imageboard, envisioning a platform with a global feed, follow feature, and profile pages.

articles.title: Anon Infatuated with Customer Care Voice
articles.body:
Anon shared a story about speaking with a customer care representative from Kalej for over 15 minutes. The representative's sweet voice and mix of Hindi and English left Anon infatuated, especially when she reacted with a red heart on his texts.

Other anons responded with varied reactions. One anon expressed skepticism about the situation, stating that customer care representatives typically don't speak that way. Another anon joked that the representative must have been flirting because Anon's "baap ke paise" were involved.

The conversation turned to college and career choices. Anon mentioned considering a low-branch college to advance next year. Another respondent advised against it, warning that it would be difficult later on. A separate discussion emerged about the possibility of getting into IIT.

Anon also received advice on how to navigate social situations, with one respondent telling him to "learn to fit in or be a lolcow." Another anon expressed difficulty reconnecting with former classmates, stating that they didn't share similar vibes.

The thread also included some crude remarks and jokes. One anon asked if it was possible to "bang" the customer care representative. Others made comments about Anon's chances with her.

The discussion concluded with anons offering differing opinions on college choices, relationships, and social interactions. No clear consensus emerged, but the conversation remained focused on personal experiences and advice.

articles.title: Anon Speculates on IAC's Physical Appearance
articles.body:
Anon started a thread with a seemingly innocuous question about wedding venues, but it quickly devolved into speculation about the physical appearance of IAC, the writer of the East of Inch newspaper.

One anon imagined IAC as 'a tall slender indian girl, short pixie hair sitting on her desk working on her website.' Another anon disagreed, saying IAC's facial features would be 'sharp' and not have 'the droopyness of gobar.' Anon pictured IAC as having 'beige to brown' skin, while another anon expressed a preference for not imagining IAC as a woman, saying 'I dont want to be reminded that hes a chad.' A respondent shot back with an obscene remark, telling another anon to stop imagining IAC as a woman.

The speculation got more outlandish, with one anon guessing IAC could be 'a 6'2 bhachad.' Anon also threw around insults and pejoratives, including 'gobar,' 'bhenchod,' and 'jeet.' Anon's exchange reveals more about the community's preoccupations than any fact about IAC.

articles.title: Anon Apologizes for Anti-Dalit Posts, Receives Backlash
articles.body:
Anon posted an apology for previous anti-dalit remarks, stating they were misled by Twitter alt-right accounts and had wrongly dehumanized the dalit community. The original poster expressed regret for making fun of dalits' past.

Another anon responded, claiming that dalits from certain regions like UP and Bihar are poor and oppressed, but also acknowledged that some dalits can be hateful towards upper castes.

This poster made a distinction between dalits from their region and those from other areas. Several anons reacted negatively to the apology.

One called the OP a "pussy" for backing down and reinforced the idea that dalits are inherently violent and cannot be civilized. Another anon stated that the fault lies not with dalits but with liberals who advocate for reservation and social justice.

The discussion quickly turned hostile, with multiple anons expressing extreme hatred towards dalits. One anon suggested that the existence of dalits is a problem, while another made a genocidal remark about scalping and burning dalits.

Some anons also engaged in side conversations, discussing topics like real estate and genetics. One anon disagreed with the idea that upper-caste liberals can change their views, suggesting that political orientation has a genetic component.

The thread was marked by intense hostility towards dalits, with several anons using derogatory language and expressing violent sentiments.

articles.title: Anon slams 'pajeeta' women, sparks debate
articles.body:
Anon posted that 'pajeeta' women spend their husbands' or fathers' money extravagantly and labelled them as materialistic, comparing them to Jews but describing them as 'smelly and low IQ'.

Another anon responded by criticizing those who support 'fanatic politicians', calling them 'opputunist pigs'.

A respondent claimed that North Indian women, in particular, are 'low IQ and Woke', while another stated that they would support Congress if Sanjay Gandhi were still alive.

The discussion also touched on caste, with one poster referencing 'Dalit shitskin' and others using similar slurs. Anon questioned the original poster's basis for their claims, asking about a woman's qualifications and skill set.

articles.title: Anon proposes new imageboard features
articles.body: Anon posted a list of features for a proposed new imageboard, including a global feed, follow feed, like and bookmark features, and AI-powered post categorization. Other Anons responded with skepticism and jokes, one calling the proposal "vibecoding" and another suggesting the creator make a forum site instead. One respondent suggested bringing back a video-sharing platform with a twist, using webtorrent to stream videos from users' PCs.
